theory.canonical_statement: ho} F_{beta}{}^{rho} pass # (Use tensor libraries to avoid manual index mistakes.) Notes: • For index manipulations, using a tensor CAS (xAct for Mathematica, einsteinpy or sympy.tensor) avoids index-order errors. • Keep expressions symbolic as long as possible; only substitute numeric parameters (e.g., fixed rotation rate $\omega$) at the end. 9. Simplifications for slow rotation / near-field If rotation speed is nonrelativistic, expand in small parameter $\beta = |\omega\times r|/c$ and keep up to $\mathcal{O}(\beta)$ or $\mathcal{O}(\beta^2)$ as needed. Provide these approximate forms for $T^{00},T^{0i},T^{ij}$ for readability and physical interpretation. 10. Checklist / deliverables you can request next • Full symbolic expansion of $T^{\mu\nu}$ components using the exact metric and $A_{\rm total}$ from your notes (I will substitute your explicit metric and potential and produce the long component expressions). • A runnable SymPy notebook (.ipynb) that computes $T^{\mu\nu}$, performs the 3+1 decomposition at sample points, and diagonalizes $S^i{}_j$. • A first-order expansion in $\beta$ with simplifie
theory.technical_interpretation: Relation: T^00,T^0i,T^ij is a symbolic expression, written T^00,T^0i,T^ij. It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from T, j. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
